plugins.name: Pratyakṣa Context Engineering Harness
plugins.description: Long-context discipline for Claude Code: typed (Avacchedaka) limitor conditions on every retrieval, sublation (bādha) of contradicted claims, witness-consciousness (Sākṣī) invariants enforced as system messages, event-boundary compaction, and Khyātivāda hallucination classification — surfaced as 15 MCP tools, 3 skills, 3 agents, 4 slash commands, and 3 lifecycle hooks. Built on Vedic epistemology, validated on RULER, HELMET, NoCha, HaluEval, TruthfulQA, FACTS-Grounding, and SWE-bench Verified.

setup.notes: Install uv (one-time, ~5 MB). Restart Claude Code. Python deps and the optional Qwen3 surprise model load lazily on first use — no venv, pip, or `claude mcp add` needed.

features.name: Avacchedaka-typed retrieval
features.description: Every retrieval is gated by a typed (qualificand, qualifier, condition) limitor — no implicit context drift across topics.

features.name: Sublation of contradicted claims
features.description: Bādha-style soft delete: when a higher-precision element contradicts an older one, the older element's precision drops to 0.0 but its provenance is preserved for audit.

features.name: Witness-consciousness invariants (Sākṣī)
features.description: Stable per-session invariants are pushed as a real Claude `system` field both stages of the Manas/Buddhi flow — never inlined into user content.

features.name: Event-boundary compaction
features.description: Token-level surprise (vLLM/HF/heuristic backend) detects discourse boundaries and triggers scoped store compression instead of indiscriminate truncation.

features.name: Khyātivāda hallucination classification
features.description: 7-class Vedic error taxonomy (Anyathā, Ātma, Akhyāti, Asat, Anirvacanīya, Viparīta, none) with few-shot Claude classifier and rule-based guardrails.

features.name: 15 MCP tools + 3 skills + 3 agents + 4 commands + 3 lifecycle hooks
features.description: Self-contained Claude Code plugin — no dependency on attractor-flow, ralph-loop, vllm, or mlflow.
